c_signature: int mpfr_round_near_x (mpfr_ptr y, mpfr_srcptr v, mpfr_uexp_t err, int dir, mpfr_rnd_t rnd)
doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_round_near_x -- the 'can we round an approximation early?' helper used throughout the transcendental functions (the MPFR_FAST_COMPUTE_IF_SMALL_INPUT wrapper). SUBSTRATE class: it is an internal helper, not a public op, and depends on the substrate primitives mpfr_round_p and the MPFR_RNDRAW_GEN macro (= round_raw_generic); the runner's requireCoreImport AST gate is exempt (eval/harness/runner.ts L1188) and the production port belongs in src/internal/mpfr/, NOT src/ops/.

Contract (round_near_x.c L26-L45): assuming y = o(f(x)) = o(x + g(x)) with |g(x)| < 2^(EXP(v)-err), and x small enough that f(x) ~= v, this decides whether v can be rounded to prec(y) bits to give the correctly-rounded y. v must be a non-singular value (NOT NaN/Inf/Zero) -- the C asserts this (round_near_x.c L164). dir is the direction of the error term: dir=0 => error is toward zero (f(x) < x), dir=1 => error is away from zero (f(x) > x). Returns 0 if it CANNOT round (and leaves y UNMODIFIED); otherwise returns the ternary flag, which is never 0 (an exact value cannot be detected here -- round_near_x.c L44, L231-L233).

Algorithm (round_near_x.c L154-L235):
  1. RNDF -> treated as RNDZ (L161-L162).
  2. Cannot-round test (L171-L177): returns 0 unless err > prec(y)+1 AND (err > prec(v) OR mpfr_round_p(MANT(v), LIMB_SIZE(v), err, prec(y)+(rnd==RNDN))). When this fails, y is untouched.
  3. Otherwise round v into y with the round-raw machinery (L179-L193): set EXP(y)=EXP(v), SIGN(y)=SIGN(v), then MPFR_RNDRAW_GEN; the truncate/addoneulp branches feed the error-direction fixups.
  4. inexact==0 fixups (L199-L229): if setting v in y was exact, apply the error term -- for dir=0 (error toward zero) under a like-RNDZ mode, do nexttozero(y) (and set underflow if y becomes 0); for dir=1 (error away) under a like-RNDA mode, do nexttoinf(y) (and set overflow if y becomes Inf).

Ternary semantics: the returned ret is the sign of (rounded - exact-f(x)); it is always +-1 or +-2 (the round-raw inexact), normalised in this port to a JS number ret in {-1, 1} for the rounded cases and 0 for the cannot-round case. (In practice the observed return for the rounding cases is +-1; the +-2 even-rounding values are folded by the engine before return.)
divergence_from_c:
1. Return shape: C writes y in place and returns int. The TS port returns a struct { value: MPFR | null, ret: number }. When the C returns 0 (cannot round, y untouched), the port returns { value: null, ret: 0 } -- the null value is the explicit 'y was not modified' signal, replacing the C convention of leaving the caller's y unchanged. When the C returns non-zero, the port returns { value: <rounded y>, ret: <ternary> }.
2. yprec is passed explicitly (the precision of the destination y); in C this is carried by the y mpfr_t the caller pre-allocated. The golden constructs y at yprec and reports it via the inputs.
3. err is passed as a bigint (decoded from a decimal-string wire); it is an mpfr_uexp_t (unsigned exponent) in C. dir is a JS number 0/1 (jl_kv_int). rnd is a RoundingMode string.
4. Global flag side effects (underflow on nexttozero-to-0, overflow on nexttoinf-to-Inf, and the old_flags save/restore at L159/L208) are NOT surfaced in the struct return. Under the default exponent range the golden does not drive y to 0 or Inf, so these flag paths are not exercised; if they were, the value would still be the post-fixup y.
5. Precondition: v must be non-singular (the C asserts !MPFR_IS_SINGULAR(v)). The golden only emits normal v. The port may assume it (substrate hot path) or throw on a singular v; the golden does not test singular v.
6. RNDF is mapped to RNDZ (round_near_x.c L161-L162); the golden does not exercise RNDF (the public RoundingMode union is the five non-RNDF modes).
refs: mpfr/src/round_near_x.c L26-L45 -- the contract comment (y = o(x+g(x)), err/dir semantics, 'returns 0 if it can't round').
mpfr/src/round_p.c -- mpfr_round_p, the cannot-round predicate dependency (already ported: src/internal/mpfr/round_p.ts).
mpfr/src/mpfr-impl.h L1836-L1964 -- MPFR_RNDRAW_GEN / MPFR_RNDRAW, the round-raw machinery used at L183.
mpfr/src/round_raw_generic.c -- the rounding engine behind MPFR_RNDRAW_GEN (sibling golden eval/functions/mpfr_round_raw/).
src/internal/mpfr/round_raw.ts -- the single-bigint roundMantissa, usable for the round-into-y step.
eval/functions/mpfr_round_p/golden_driver.c -- sibling substrate driver; extern-decl of a __MPFR_DECLSPEC symbol.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' -- ternary is sign of (rounded - exact); v must be non-singular; RNDF folds to RNDZ.
